page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4a family. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, aligning it with compute-optimized resource distributions.

The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. It lacks GPU accelerators, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning workloads. Additionally, the instance does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n4a-highcpu-64 provides high compute density for workloads that require substantial processing power without a corresponding demand for large memory footprints. This profile is suitable for compute-heavy tasks such as batch processing, distributed analytics, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines where memory consumption remains within the 2.0 GB per core threshold.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the n4a family, configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. This resource allocation provides a compute-centric 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance features no local storage and lacks GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence and limiting its use to non-accelerated workloads. It also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the server offers high compute density, making it a viable option for CPU-bound tasks that do not demand large memory footprints. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage or GPU accelerators, designed for CPU-intensive workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server configuration featuring sixty-four dedicated virtual CPUs and a lean two gigabytes of memory per core.
bullet_points: 128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for CPU-bound workloads and batch processing
